title: SSA Reasoner – Step Ordering Delta
description: Delta-style schema for proposed step reordering within SSA sessions. Used by the Reasoner to suggest re-sequencing, pinning, or parallelizing steps across cooking, cleaning, garden, animals, preservation, and storehouse domains.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version for this payload shape.

domains.description: Domains this delta applies to. Typically a subset of ['cooking', 'cleaning', 'garden', 'animals', 'preservation', 'storehouse'].

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this step ordering delta was generated.

requestId.description: Optional ID that ties this delta back to a Reasoner request.

householdId.description: Optional household/profile identifier this delta is scoped to.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale in which labels and notes are expressed, e.g. 'en-US'.

reorderings.description: List of individual reordering proposals to add/update/remove.

id.description: Stable identifier for this reordering rule or recommendation. May reference an existing rule for updates.

status.description: Whether this reordering is newly proposed, already accepted, or deprecated.

operation.description: How this delta should be applied to the step-ordering knowledge.

sessionId.description: Target session id whose steps are being reordered. Must match an SSA Session.id.

sessionDomain.description: Domain of the target session. Helps the engine route the recommendation.

label.description: Short, user-facing label for this reordering, e.g. 'Front-load chopping tasks'.

originalOrder.description: Original step ordering by step id, as seen when the Reasoner was invoked.

proposedOrder.description: Proposed new ordering of step ids for this session. Must contain only ids from originalOrder (or the session's step ids) unless introducing isAllowedExtraSteps.

changeMagnitude.description: Rough description of how disruptive this reordering is.

confidence.description: 0–1 numeric confidence score representing how safe/beneficial this reordering is.

drivers.description: Key factors driving this reordering recommendation, normalized to 0–1 where possible.

prepEfficiency.description: 0–1 score for how much this reordering improves prep/logistics efficiency.

equipmentUtilization.description: 0–1 score for how much this reordering improves equipment utilization (e.g. keeping oven or stove consistently busy).

overlapTimersReduction.description: 0–1 metric for how much this reordering reduces conflicting timers or complex overlaps.

quietHoursCompliance.description: 0–1 score for how much this reordering improves compliance with quiet hours (1 = fully compliant).

sabbathCompliance.description: 0–1 score for how much this reordering improves Sabbath compliance (1 = fully compliant).

yieldOrQualityImpact.description: Estimated impact on yield/quality from applying this reordering. Range -1.0 (significant degradation) to 1.0 (significant improvement).

constraints.description: Ordering constraints that must hold when applying this reordering.

mustPrecede.description: List of step id pairs [A, B] where A must come before B.

mustFollow.description: List of step id pairs [A, B] where A must come after B.

pinnedSteps.description: Step ids that must remain in their original position or near it (e.g. Sabbath boundary, critical food-safety step).

maxParallelSteps.description: Soft limit on how many steps should be in-flight concurrently when using this ordering.

parallelization.description: Optional grouping and concurrency hints derived from the new ordering.

parallelGroups.description: Each group represents steps that can safely run in parallel (subject to equipment and attention constraints).

stepIds.description: Step ids that can run in parallel.

attentionLoad.description: 0–1 estimate of how demanding this group is for the user’s attention (1 = highly demanding).

equipmentIds.description: Optional list of equipment identifiers if tracked by the domain engine.

notes.description: User-facing note for why these steps are grouped in parallel.

sequentialIsRecommended.description: True if the Reasoner recommends running all steps sequentially despite potential parallelism.

notes.description: User-facing explanation of the reordering and its implications, suitable for display in the SessionRunner UI.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this reordering record was first created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this reordering record was last updated.

guardResult.description: Result of a single guard evaluation (e.g. Sabbath, weather, inventory, quiet hours, battery).

ok.description: True if this guard passed; false if it failed or is blocking.

severity.description: Severity level for a non-ok guard.

suggestedAction.description: Suggested next step if this guard is not ok.

guardSummary.description: Aggregate summary of multiple guard results for a given session/step ordering.

okToApply.description: True only if all blocking guards passed for this reordering.

hasWarnings.description: True if one or more guards emitted warnings.

blockingGuards.description: List of guardIds that are currently blocking the reordering.

warningGuards.description: List of guardIds that are warning-only.

results.description: Per-guard results used to compute this summary.
